title: showTagline is ignored when animation is disabled
reasoning: The component exposes showTagline as its own prop and defaults it to true, but the render condition also requires animated to be true. A caller that intentionally disables animation for reduced motion or a static placement cannot still show the tagline, even with showTagline={true}. This makes the showTagline prop only partially effective and couples content visibility to animation state.
reproduction: Render <BrandLogo animated={false} showTagline={true} /> and observe that the tagline is omitted.
recommendation: Render the tagline based on showTagline alone, and gate only animation-specific styling on animated if needed.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are provided for this component, and there is no assertion covering the interaction between animated and showTagline.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component test that renders BrandLogo with animated={false} and showTagline={true}, then asserts the tagline text is present while animation-only halo effects are absent.
minimumFixScope: Update the conditional around the tagline in frontend/src/components/BrandLogo.tsx and add a focused render test for the prop combination.
